description_long: This gatehouse is a large, rectangular stone structure featuring two prominent towers at either end, each topped with a domed roof and a golden spire. The central section is an open colonnade with a series of evenly spaced arches, allowing passage beneath. The towers have multiple levels, with the uppermost sections containing small, arched windows and decorative cornices. The main entryway is a grand, arched opening set into one of the towers, while the other tower features a large, open archway at its base. The exterior is finished in a brown, stone-like material, giving it a historic and fortified appearance. The overall design suggests a blend of classical and medieval architectural influences, with symmetrical elements and robust construction.
description: A large, brown stone gatehouse with two domed towers, arched entryways, and a central colonnade, exuding a historic and fortified look.
description_view_0: Front view showing both towers, the central colonnade with arches, and the main arched entryway on the right tower.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the left tower, the domed roof, and the layered architectural details.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with both towers and the central colonnade visible, but the main entryway is less prominent.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the right tower, its open archway, and the domed roof with spire.
attribute_entryway_design: Large arched stone entryway integrated into the tower base.
attribute_window_placement: Small arched windows encircle the upper levels of both towers.
